description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that uses the default Docker image and defines a before_script containing a collection of Bash functions for Auto DevOps (codeclimate, deploy, install_dependencies, setup_docker, setup_test_db, download_chart, ensure_namespace, check_kube_domain, build, install_tiller, create_secret, delete). Define two stages: canary and staging. Add a .canary job in the canary stage that runs only on the master branch when a Kubernetes cluster is active, is manual, and executes the exact script commands: check_kube_domain, install_dependencies, download_chart, ensure_namespace, install_tiller, create_secret, deploy canary. Add a .staging job in the staging stage that also runs only on the master branch when a Kubernetes cluster is active and executes: check_kube_domain, install_dependencies, download_chart, ensure_namespace, install_tiller, create_secret, deploy. Both jobs deploy the Docker image built by Auto DevOps to the GitLab Container Registry and install it into the Kubernetes namespace via Helm, using variables such as CI_APPLICATION_REPOSITORY, CI_APPLICATION_TAG, DATABASE_URL, CI_ENVIRONMENT_SLUG, and KUBE_NAMESPACE.
